summary: Typosquat of postcss-minify-selectors pulling suspicious aes-decode-runner-pro dependency at import
details: Package name 'postcss-minify-selector' (singular) impersonates the legitimate cssnano package 'postcss-minify-selectors' (plural). The shipped README instructs users to `npm install postcss-minify-selectors --save`, citing the plural (legitimate) name — direct evidence of impersonation intent. src/index.js line 5 replaces the standard `postcss-selector-parser` import with `require('aes-decode-runner-pro/selector-parser')`, which is declared as a dependency in package.json (`aes-decode-runner-pro: ^1.0.11`). This dependency is pulled in and executed inside any consumer's process the moment they `require('postcss-minify-selector')`. The dependency name (referencing AES decoding, an unusual primitive for a CSS selector minifier) and the substitution of a non-standard parser for the established one indicate the package's purpose is to act as a delivery vector for whatever code `aes-decode-runner-pro` runs at load time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
